original: Battle of Zama: Hannibal Barca and the Carthaginian army are defeated by Roman legions under Scipio Africanus, ending the Second Punic War
The Vandals, led by King Gaiseric, take the city of Carthage in North Africa
Pope Deusdedit [Adeodatus I] elected to succeed Boniface IV as Catholic Pope
Abbot Humbert of Echternach Abbey opens the grave of Saint Willibrord, founder of the abbey in the 7th century
Rindfleisch Persecutions -140 Jews of Heilbron, Germany are murdered
French retake Bordeaux following the Battle of Castillon
The Thirteen Years' War ends with the Second Peace of Thorn, Germany
Ethiopian Emperor Dawit II receives a Portuguese diplomatic mission at his camp in the Ethiopian Highlands, who regard him as Prester John, the legendary king of a lost Christian nation
Dutch provinces begin consultation about Spanish existence
In Boston the 1st general court is held
Beach Island in North Sea destroyed by a heavy storm flood
English Lord Shaftesbury flees to Holland
French C. Hopffer patents the automatic fire extinguisher in England
England declares war on Spain [OS=Oct 30]
Stamp Act Congress meets in New York City, writes the Declaration of Rights and Grievances
Edict of Toleration issued by Holy Roman Emperor Joseph II, providing limited freedom of worship for non-Roman Catholic Christians
US Government and Chickasaw Indians sign a treaty
In Parnaíba; Simplício Dias da Silva, João Cândido de Deus e Silva and Domingos Dias declare the independent state of Piauí
First flour mill in Hawaii begins operations
Wilhelm Tempel discovers diffuse nebula around Pleid star Merope
Battle of Buckland Mills, Virginia
Approx 25 Confederates make surprise attack on St Albans, Vermont
Battle of Cedar Creek, Virginia, Union beats back Confederate attackers
US Brigadier General Emory Upton (25) promoted to Major General
1st African Americans (4) elected to US House of Representatives
British steamship SS Cambria wrecked off the north-west of Ireland with the loss of 178 lives
Afghan's emir Mohammed Yakub forced to resign
Moshav Gederah is attacked by Arabs
Painter Henry Ossawa Tanner wins Medal of Honor at Paris Exposition
Alberto Santos-Dumont proves airships are maneuverable by circling the Eiffel Tower in his airship No. 6, winning the Deutsch Prize and 100,000 francs
Polytechnic University of the Philippines founded as Manila Business School through superintendence of American C.A. O'Reilley
1st printing of Dutch Tribune (Wijnkoop/Van Ravesteyn/Ceton)
Royal Mint in London sends dies for Canadian $1 coin to Ottawa Branch
Tripoli (Libya) passes from Turkish to Italian control
Russia and Italy declare war on Bulgaria
US bankers arrange a $500 million loan to the British and French
US Army opens Love Field, a military airplane pilot training center, in Dallas, Texas
Portuguese Prime Minister António Granjo and other politicians are murdered in a Lisbon coup
Beierse government refuses to prohibit NSDAP newspaper Völkischer Beobachter
General Christian Worker's union demands 8 hr work day in Belgium
Italian army takes Somalia
John C Garand patents semi-automatic rifle
Jules Ladoumègue runs world record 1 km (2:23.6)
Austria forbids demonstration by Nazis & anti fascists
British government signs trade agreement with Soviet Union
Berlin Olympic Committee vote to introduce basketball in 1936
HR Ekins of "NY World-Telegram" beats 2 other reporters in a race around the world on commercial flights, by 18½ days
1st woman jockey in North America, Anna Lee Wiley in Mexico
Conference of foreign ministers in Moscow
Streptomycin, the first antibiotic remedy for tuberculosis, is isolated by researchers at Rutgers University in Piscataway, New Jersey
British Prime MinisterWinston Churchillflies back to London from Moscow
Canadian troops liberate Aardensburg, Netherlands
US forces land in the Philippines
US Navy says Black women can join WAVES
De Gaulles RPF wins French municipal elections
A's trade 2nd baseman Nellie Fox to White Sox for Joe Tipton
Bird Building at Cleveland Metroparks Zoo is dedicated (Cleveland, Ohio)
UN forces enter Pyongyang, capital of North Korea
US President Harry Truman formally ends state of war with Germany

Alain Bombard departs from the Canary Islands on his solitary journey across the Atlantic ocean with almost no provisions and only a sextant for navigation to test his theory that a shipwrecked person could survive
1st jet transcontinental nonstop scheduled service
Dystopian novel "Fahrenheit 451" by Ray Bradbury is published in the US
Egypt and Great Britain sign treaty; British troops depart
First ascent of Cho Oyu, sixth highest mountain in the world at 8,201 metres
KAKE TV channel 10 in Wichita, KS (ABC) begins broadcasting
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
Florence Henderson joins Today Show panel
William Gibson's "Miracle Worker" premieres in NYC
France grants Mauritania independence
KWCS (now KOOG) TV channel 30 in Ogden, UT (IND) begins broadcasting
US imposes embargo on exports to Cuba
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
American sprinter Edith McGuire runs an Olympic record 23.0 to win the women's 200m gold medal in Tokyo; minor place medallists Irena Szewińska of Poland and Australian Marilyn Black both record 23.1
In a blanket finish in the 80m hurdles at the Tokyo Olympics, Germany's Karin Balzer and Teresa Cieply of Poland both record 10.5s with Pam Kilborn of Australia 10.6; Balzer is awarded the gold medal
Tamara Press of the Soviet Union wins the women's discus with an Olympic record throw 57.27m in Tokyo; first of 2 gold medals at the Games (shot put)
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Igor Ter-Ovanesyan of USSR, sets then long jump record at 27' 4 3/4"
Mariner 5 makes fly-by of Venus

American swimmer Jan Henne in 1:00.0 leads American sweep of the women's 100m freestyle medals at the Mexico City Olympics; teammates Susan Pedersen & Linda Gustavson both swim 1:00.3 for minor medals
Australian swimmer Michael Wendon sets world record 52.2s to win the men's 100m freestyle gold medal at the Mexico City Olympics
Derry Citizen's Action Committee, formed Oct 9, stages illegal sit-down at Guildhall Square as part of large civil disobedience campaign
Golden Gate Bridge charges tolls only for southbound cars
New Zealand scores an upset victory in the men's coxed four rowing final at the Mexico City Olympics; beats favoured East German crew by 2.58s; first ever rowing gold medal for the Kiwis
The West German crew beats Australia by just 0.9s to win the men's eights rowing gold medal at the Mexico City Olympics
Oakland Darryle Lamonica passes for 6 touchdowns vs Buffalo (50-21)
Amdahl Corp forms at Sunnyvale, California
John Frazier kills Ohta's declares WW 3 has begun
A group of Northern Ireland Members of Parliament begin a 48 hour hunger strike against the policy of Internment
Last issue of "Look" magazine is published
Ulster Vanguard leader William Craig speaks at a meeting of right-wing Members of Parliament at Westminster: "We are prepared to come out and shoot and kill"
OPEC oil embargo on the United States begins as participating nations cease oil exports to the US and begin a series of production cuts
Saudi Arabia, Libya, and other Arab states proclaim an embargo on oil exports to the United States
NBA Detroit Pistons beat Trailblazers in Portland (next win 6-1-90)
NHL Detroit Red Wing Mickey Redmond scores 1st hat trick against Washington Capitals
Niue becomes self-govering, in association with New Zealand
Cleveland Browns' Don Cockroft kicks club record 5 field goals
Battle of Aishiya, Lebanon, during Lebanese Civil War
Corpse of kidnapped West German business executive Hanns Martin Schleyer is found
Supersonic jet Concorde makes its 1st landing in NYC, a passengerless test flight by Air France, two days after US Supreme Court overrules local authority's ban
Steve McPeak rides 101'9" unicycle
LA Dodgers beat Montreal Expos for NL pennant
Automaker John DeLorean arrested on cocaine charges (Not guilty)
Grenada army General Hudson Austin forms "revolutionary council"
NHL Philadelphia Flyers begin 13-game winning streak
Space Shuttle Columbia moves to Orbiter Processing Facility
US Senate establishesMartin Luther King Jr.Day as a federal holiday, observed on the third Monday in January (on or near his January 15 birthday)
USSR expels five US diplomats
Black Monday: Stock markets around the world crash, including the Dow Jones Industrial Average, which falls 508.32 points (22%), 4½ times the previous daily record
US warships destroy 2 Iranian oil platforms in Persian Gulf
3 Americans win Nobel in physics; 3 W Germans win chemistry Nobel
Britain bans broadcast interviews with IRA members
Car bomb kills 7 Israelis, wounds 11 near Lebanon border
Roxette releases "Roxette Look Sharp!" album
South African anti-apartheid leader Sisulu wins $100,000 Human Rights prize
US Senate passes a bill curbing ads during children's TV shows
Astor Piazzolla and William Finn's musical "Dangerous Games" premieres at Nederlander Theater NYC
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
HCA, Helsinki Citizens Assembly, forms in Prague
Longest NCAA football game (3:52) as Rhode Island beats Maine, 52-30 (6 OT)
Lonnie Glieberman purchased Ottawa Rough Riders from CFL
UN authorizes arms, military and police supply embargo against Haiti
160 killed in fighting in Tsjetsjenie (Chechnya)
Palestinian bomb attack on bus in Tel Aviv, kills 22
The Earth Liberation Front sets fire to Vail Mountain ski resort in Colorado, causing $12 million in damage
SIEV-X, an Indonesian fishing boat en-route to Christmas Island, carrying over 400 asylum seekers, sinks killing 353
Care International aid worker Margaret Hassan is kidnapped in Iraq
Myanmar prime minister Khin Nyunt is ousted and placed under house arrest by the SPDC on charges of corruption
Hurricane Wilma becomes the most intense Atlantic hurricane on record with a minimum pressure of 882 mb
Bomb explosion rocks Glorietta 2, a shopping mall in Makati, the Philippines killing 11, injuring more than 100 people
8 people are killed and 78 injured by a car bomb in Beirut, Lebanon
11 people are killed after a plane crashes in Namur, Belgium
16 people are killed and 30 are wounded by a suicide bombing Beledweyne, Somalia
A working human intestine is generated in a laboratory from stem cells in the United States
Scientists from the University of California find evidence that life on Earth may have begun 4.1 billion years ago, 300 million years earlier than previously thought
Outbreak of the Marburg virus declared by Uganda's Ministry of Health
"Halloween" film reboot starring Jamie Curtis makes a record $77m for a horror film with a female lead, biggest debut any film with female lead over 55 in US
Sunnyside, Queens, New York City: Intersection of 46th Street and Skillman Avenue named "Ethel Plimack Way", in honor of a longtime resident
Belgium officials say country facing a "tsunami" of COVID-19 cases amid new restrictions and figures it has third-highest number of Covid-related deaths per 100,000 people globally
Irish government moves country to highest level of COVID-19 restrictions amid rising cases
Ming handscroll painting “Ten Views of Lingbi Rock” by Wu Bin sells for 512.9 million yuan ($77 million) at auction in Beijing, a new world record for a classical Chinese work
Peru announces rediscovered 37m figure of a cat, completed 500 BC to 200 AD, a geoglyph outline in the Nazca Desert
US charges six Russian military officers with massive cyber-attack meant to disrupt 2020 Tokyo Olympics, 2017 French presidential election, and Ukraine's power grid
Moscow's mayor orders unvaccinated people over 60 years to stay home for four months amid a worsening COVID-19 crisis
Massachusetts names Podokesaurus holyokensis ("swift-footed lizard of Holyoke") as official state dinosaur

Philadelphia Phillies right-hander Aaron Nola faces his brother San Diego Padres catcher Austin Nola in first MLB playoff sibling pitcher-batter battle; Austin goes 1-2, with rally starting RBI in 8-5 win at Petco Park, San Diego
Israeli airstrike on Gaza city of Beit Lahia kills at least 87 people
King Johnof England dies at Newark-on-Trent and is succeeded by his nine-year-old sonHenry III
17-year-old English KingEdward IIIcaptures his mother's lover and the country's de facto ruler Roger Mortimer at Nottingham Castle (later has him hanged)
Christopher Columbussights "Isabela", now Fortune Island/Long Cay, Bahamas
Martin Lutherbecomes a doctor of theology (Doctor in Biblia)
Oliver Cromwellgrants the English East India Company a new unlimited charter, saving it from being broken up
Napoleon Bonaparteand his Grande Armée begin their retreat from Moscow numbering just 100,000 (started campaign with 500,000)
Richard Wagner's opera "Tannhäuser" premieres in Dresden
Pierre Savorgnan de BrazzameetsHenry Morton Stanley
South African President of TransvaalPaul Krugerdeparts for Europe
Edward Elgar's "Pomp & Circumstance March" premieres in Liverpool, England
Frederick Winslow Tayloris awarded an honorary degree of Doctor of Science by the University of Pennsylvania
At a meeting of the Natal Indian Congress (NIC) in Durban, NIC secretaries, M. C. Anglia and Dada Osman, severely criticiseMahatma Gandhiand tender their resignations
France extends formal recognition of the Provisional Government in Crete proclaimedEleftherios Venizelos
1st US Distinguished Service Medal awarded to a living female recipient,Anna Howard Shaw
MLB's American League presidentBan Johnsonpersuades team owners to prohibit boxing in their ballparks
Russian Politburo throws outLeon Trotskyand his followers
Henry Fordgives his 1st radio speech
Jimmie Foxxwins AL MVP andChuck Kleinwins NL MVP
Mao Zedong's Red Army reaches Shanxi in Northern China
Hermann Goeringbegins plunder through Nazi's occupied areas
Singer Julius LaRosa is fired on TV byArthur Godfrey
Montreal CanadienMaurice "Rocket" Richardbecomes the first NHL player to score 500 goals

Stirling Mosswins season ending Moroccan Grand Prix at Ain-Diab but fellow Brit Mike Hawthorn takes World Drivers Championship from Moss by just 1 point by finishing second; first British world champion
Martin Luther King Jr.arrested in Atlanta sit-in
Bobby Orrmakes his NHL regular-season debut for the Boston Bruins against the Detroit Red Wings
Scottish Matra-Ford driverJackie Stewartfinishes 4th in season ending Mexican Grand Prix to win his first F1 World Drivers Championship by 26 points from Jacky Ickx of Belgium
Columbia Records releases "Jonathan Livingston Seagull", the ninth studio album byNeil Diamond; the Grammy Award-winning soundtrack album is more popular than the film itself
Ringo Starrreleases music single "Photograph" in the UK

Virginia Slims Circuit WTA Tour Tennis Championship, Los Angeles Sports Arena; Australian Evonne Goolagong wins her first title, beating AmericanChris Evert6-3, 6-4;Billie Jean KingandRosemary Casalswin Doubles
US PresidentGerald Fordsigns 1st major revision of copyright law since 1909
Nicolaas Bloembergen and ArthurSchawlowwin Nobel Prize for Physics (laser)
Woody Woodward resigns as NY Yankee GM, Lou Piniella is named GM andBilly Martinis named NY Yankee manager for 5th & final time
"Dances with Wolves" directed byKevin Costnerand starring Kevin Costner and Mary McDonnell premieres in Washington, D.C. (Academy Awards Best Picture 1991)
Cherreleases the single "Believe" (Billboard Song of the Year, 1999; Grammy Award Best Dance Recording, 2000)
Mother Teresaof Calcutta is beatified by PopeJohn Paul II
Saddam Husseingoes on trial in Baghdad for crimes against humanity
Patrick Kanescores his first NHL goal against José Théodore of the Colorado Avalanche
Decca Records releases "The Union", a collaborative album betweenElton Johnand Leon Russell, in the US; it becomes the highest charting album for both artists in over 30 years
Canadian federal elections:Justin Trudeau's Liberal Party win a majority with 184 seats
Third US Presidential debate:Donald Trumpnotably refuses to say if he will accept the result of election during debate withHillary Clintonat Nevada University, Las Vegas
New Zealand Labour Party forms a coalition government led byJacinda Ardern(37), youngest NZ leader in 161 years
Chilean PresidentSebastián Piñeradeclares a 15 day state of emergency after widespread and violent protests over rise in public transport costs
UK parliament votes for the Letwin amendment in a special Saturday sitting, which forcesBoris Johnsonto ask the EU for an extension and delays vote on his Brexit deal
US Democratic representativeAlexandria Ocasio-CortezendorsesBernie Sandersfor president

39th Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductees:Mary J. Blige;Cher;Dave MatthewsBand; Foreigner; Peter Frampton; Kool & The Gang;Ozzy Osbourne; A Tribe Called Quest; Alexis Korner; John Mayall; Big Mama Thornton;Jimmy Buffett; MC5;Dionne Warwick; and Norman Whitfield
Charlotte Brontë's novel "Jane Eyre" is published by Smith, Elder & Co. of London under her pen name "Currer Bell"
